message:
test(fuse): assert-then-bail in getfssysfspath dispatch test

Restructures dispatch_ioctl_getfssysfspath_encodes_129_byte_struct_
with_fixture_path so it exercises the assert-and-return idiom that
let-else can't express directly. The test still validates the same
contract — the dispatcher routes FS_IOC_GETFSSYSFSPATH to FsOps,
encodes the 129-byte fs_sysfs_path struct, and records the call —
but the failure mode now surfaces through a normal assert! at the
top with a legible message before falling through to the let-else
which short-circuits via return on the unreachable mismatch arm.

Functionally equivalent to the prior panic!() form for the
happy-path case. The only observable difference is which line
fails when something regresses: the matches!() assertion fires
first and produces the same diagnostic context the original
panic! did.
